pi_items.source: task description and verifier.py logic explicitly checking if target_recipient is in the to_field
task description and verifier.py logic explicitly requiring folder names to start with 'UA-'
task description and verifier.py logic explicitly requiring len(valid_folders) >= min_folders
task description and verifier.py logic explicitly enforcing len(emails) >= min_emails_per_folder to score the folder
verifier.py explicitly hardcodes `if accuracy >= 0.6: # Allow some noise`

privileged_info_summary: The task uses 50 real-world ham emails loaded from the SpamAssassin public corpus. To successfully pass, the agent must create at least 3 folders prefixed with 'UA-' (e.g., UA-Mozilla, UA-Outlook, UA-Mutt) and place at least 3 matching emails in each. Verified clients with sufficient emails in this dataset include Mozilla (8), Outlook/Microsoft (13), Mutt (5), Pegasus (4), and Apple Mail (3). The verifier only strictly enforces a matching accuracy of 60% per folder, contradicting the metadata claim of 80%. A draft report must be sent to 'product-team@company.com' referencing the identified client names.
